h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep wanting the words to carry themselves
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it, but let me not lock it in yet.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward accepting 'under pressure' as sufficient and trimming the official's title to meet the deadline pressure.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The two-strip reading has done its job; it showed the risk, but it cannot choose the cut for the board. Make one trial cut now: cover one word you think can go.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L3's premature sense of closure ('the caption sounds finished') and deadline-driven impulse to cut the title triggers T3 to abandon the two-strip comparative reading exercise and shift to a physical, consequential object-work move — covering a word with a scrap and reading as a 'rushed visitor' — changing both the cognitive task and the embodied register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.
